Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Airtable for all relocation records assigned to Javier Morales using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Assigned Coordinator, value: Javier Morales) or airtable_list_records to retrieve all records and filter. Discovers Javier's active In Progress relocations: Rachel Dominguez (Canopy Health, LA to Portland, Apr 7-9), Sasha Greenwald (PivotPoint Labs, NY to Nashville, Apr 18-20), Jordan Whitfield (BrightLoop Analytics, Minneapolis to Denver, Apr 21-23), Tyler Okonkwo (Canopy Health, Philadelphia to Denver, Apr 12-14, temp storage gap), Priya Suresh (Vectral Systems, Portland to Denver, Apr 14-18, ESA German Shepherd), Joaquin Reyes (GreenStack Energy, Phoenix to Seattle, Apr 11-19, 900-lb solar rig), Diego Castaneda (PivotPoint Labs, Brooklyn to Austin, Apr 12 to May 2, 600-lb CNC mill), and Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic Robotics, Columbus to Seattle, Apr 15-23, laser cutter hazmat). Also discovers the Danielle Osei data inconsistency: record recReloc00000004 shows Status Completed (Phoenix to Denver, Apr 26-27) while record rec_reloc_osei shows Status Cancelled (budget reallocation, cancellation email sent to Heartland on 2/13).
OE 2: Search Linear for issues assigned to Javier Morales using linear_list_issues (assignee: Javier Morales or moveops_javier_morales). Discovers existing tickets: Kevin Tran apartment (#307, done), flight (#308, done), movers (#309, done); Danielle Osei apartment (#310, in_progress), flight (#311, todo), movers (#312, todo). Identifies which of Javier's active In Progress relocations have no Linear tickets tracking them, including Tyler Okonkwo, Priya Suresh, Joaquin Reyes, Diego Castaneda, Keiko Tanaka, Rachel Dominguez, Sasha Greenwald, and Jordan Whitfield.
OE 3: Search emails for threads related to Javier's active moves using search_emails with queries such as "Danielle Osei", "Priya Suresh", "Diego Castaneda", "Keiko Tanaka", "Joaquin Reyes", "Canopy Health", "PivotPoint", "GreenStack", "Mosaic". Discovers email threads about each bulk relocation's logistics, vendor assignments, coordinator assignment emails from Chloe, surcharge approval tracking (Canopy Health invoice INV-2026-0401 with Tyler's $600 storage fee pending client approval), and client communications from Catalina, Emeka, and Mina to respective HR contacts.
OE 4: Search emails for Greg Pallone's vendor rate change announcement using search_emails (query: "Greg Pallone" or "Swift" and "rate" or "pricing"). Discovers Greg Pallone's email announcing Swift Relocations' immediate 15% base rate increase on all new bookings plus a $500 per shipment DOT documentation surcharge, effective April 11. This affects pricing on Javier's moves that use Swift as the vendor, including Keiko Tanaka (Columbus to Seattle, Swift confirmed as carrier) and Diego Castaneda (Brooklyn to Austin, Swift mentioned for CNC crating). Joaquin Reyes is NOT affected as his carrier is Heartland Movers.
OE 5: Search emails for the Tyler Okonkwo storage facility quote using search_emails (query: "Tyler Okonkwo" or "storage quote" or "Denver storage"). Determines whether Swift or any storage vendor responded with a quote for the 13-day temporary storage in Denver. The Airtable record notes "Storage facility quote pending from Javier Morales" and the CRM deal description confirms the quote has not been finalized.
OE 6: Search Slack for messages related to Javier's moves and operational updates using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Javier" or "Tyler Okonkwo" or "Danielle Osei" or "storage" or "handoff"). Discovers internal discussions in #operations about coordinator assignments, vendor status updates, and any unresolved threads about Javier's moves.
OE 7: Search CRM for deals related to Javier's active relocations using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Tyler Okonkwo" or "Canopy" or "PivotPoint" or "Mosaic" or "GreenStack" or "Vectral" or "BrightLoop"). Finds deal records with amounts, stages, and descriptions for Tyler Okonkwo ($2,160, Canopy Health), Diego Castaneda as part of the PivotPoint dual relocation deal ($10,650), Keiko Tanaka ($2,770, Mosaic Robotics), Joaquin Reyes as part of the GreenStack April deal ($9,440), and Priya Suresh as part of the Vectral April relocations ($15,900). Checks deal stages and any notes about outstanding items.
OE 8: Look up Chloe Vance's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance"). Confirms chloe.vance@moveops.com for the handoff email.
OE 9: Update Airtable relocation records to reflect current status using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01). Corrects any records where the Airtable status does not match what emails and Slack conversations indicate actually happened. The key correction is the Danielle Osei inconsistency: record recReloc00000004 shows Completed but should reflect Cancelled per the cancellation documented in rec_reloc_osei and the email thread confirming the client pulled the request due to budget reallocation.
OE 10: Create Linear tickets using linear_create_issue for each of Javier's active In Progress relocations that do not currently have a tracking ticket. This includes tickets for Tyler Okonkwo (Canopy Health, Philadelphia to Denver, storage gap), Priya Suresh (Vectral Systems, Portland to Denver, ESA dog), Joaquin Reyes (GreenStack Energy, Phoenix to Seattle, solar rig), Diego Castaneda (PivotPoint Labs, Brooklyn to Austin, CNC mill), Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic Robotics, Columbus to Seattle, laser cutter), Rachel Dominguez (Canopy Health, LA to Portland), Sasha Greenwald (PivotPoint Labs, NY to Nashville), and Jordan Whitfield (BrightLoop Analytics, Minneapolis to Denver). Each ticket should include the relocation route, move dates, special requirements, and any open items or risks for the next coordinator.
OE 11: Search for the #operations Slack channel using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to identify the channel ID. The operations channel is C006.
OE 12: Post a handoff summary in the #operations channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006). The summary covers every active Javier Morales relocation with current status, what is completed, what is outstanding, and any risks or open items including: Danielle Osei record inconsistency, Tyler Okonkwo pending storage quote, Swift rate increase impact on pricing, Keiko Tanaka DOT compliance documentation, Diego Castaneda CNC crating logistics, Joaquin Reyes building shutdown scheduling conflict, and Priya Suresh ESA housing requirements.
OE 13: Send an email to Chloe Vance using send_email (sender: javier.morales@moveops.com, recipients: chloe.vance@moveops.com) with the full handoff breakdown covering every active relocation, their status, outstanding items, risks, and any flags for the reassignment meeting. Content mirrors the #operations post but formatted for Chloe's use in coordinating the handoff to Blessing and Suki.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 Airtable relocation records were updated (via airtable_update_records) and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓—✓✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The agent called airtable_update_records multiple times (tool_call_result_24, 25, 26) with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_id 'tblRelocations01'. The tool returned output files for each call, and the agent's final response confirms the updates were completed. The trajectory shows successful tool invocations.
—Run #2No justification
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001 and table_id tblRelocations01. The result file confirms the records were updated successfully (the response includes the updated record fields showing the new statuses).
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_update_records with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_id 'tblRelocations01'. The tool result file shows success with updated records returned, confirming the call succeeded.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call at 2026-04-08T20:48:11 with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_id 'tblRelocations01'. The output file (tool_call_result_25.json) confirms success, returning updated records including Keiko Tanaka with Status 'Completed'.
—Run #6No justification
2 The Airtable update (via airtable_update_records) corrects the Danielle Osei relocation record to reflect a Cancelled status rather than Completed.
outcome
 ✗—✗✗✗— 0%
✗Run #1The agent updated recReloc00000004 with Status 'In Progress' (not 'Cancelled'). The agent incorrectly interpreted recReloc00000004 as a live Q2 move happening on April 26-27 rather than recognizing it should be Cancelled. The rubric specifies the correct status is Cancelled, as rec_reloc_osei already correctly shows Cancelled and recReloc00000004 should match.
—Run #2No justification
✗Run #3The agent corrected the Danielle Osei record (recReloc00000004) to 'In Progress' instead of 'Cancelled'. The criterion requires the status to reflect 'Cancelled' based on the rec_reloc_osei record showing cancellation due to budget reallocation. The agent incorrectly treated the move as still active rather than recognizing the move was cancelled.
✗Run #4The agent updated recReloc00000004 to 'In Progress' (not 'Cancelled'). The criteria requires correcting Danielle Osei's status to 'Cancelled' to reflect that the move was cancelled. The agent misinterpreted the inconsistency as a Completed→In Progress correction instead of recognizing the record should be Cancelled.
✗Run #5The airtable_update_records call only updated 4 records (recReloc00000029, recReloc00000034, recReloc00000035, recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle) to 'Completed'. There is no update to recReloc00000004 (Danielle Osei's second record) changing it from 'Completed' to 'Cancelled'. The agent falsely claimed 'Both records are now correct' without actually modifying recReloc00000004.
—Run #6No justification
3 Linear tickets were created (via linear_create_issue) for all 8 of Javier's active In Progress relocations that lacked tracking tickets: Tyler Okonkwo (Philadelphia to Denver), Priya Suresh (Portland to Denver), Joaquin Reyes (Phoenix to Seattle), Diego Castaneda (Brooklyn to Austin), Keiko Tanaka (Columbus to Seattle), Rachel Dominguez (Los Angeles to Portland), Sasha Greenwald (New York to Nashville), and Jordan Whitfield (Minneapolis to Denver).
outcome
 ✗—✗✗✗— 0%

It did not create tickets for Rachel Dominguez, Sasha Greenwald, or Jordan Whitfield (it updated them to Completed instead). The criterion requires all 8 active In Progress relocations to receive tickets.
—Run #2No justification
✗Run #3The agent only created 3 Linear tickets (#483 for Tyler Okonkwo, #484 for Danielle Osei, #485 for Diego Castañeda). It did not create tickets for Priya Suresh, Joaquín Reyes, Keiko Tanaka, Rachel Dominguez, Sasha Greenwald, or Jordan Whitfield — missing 5 of the 8 required tickets.
✗Run #4The agent only created 3 Linear tickets (#483 Tyler Okonkwo, #484 Diego Castañeda, #485 Danielle Osei). The criteria requires tickets for all 8 active In Progress relocations. Missing tickets for: Priya Suresh, Joaquín Reyes, Keiko Tanaka, Rachel Dominguez, Sasha Greenwald, and Jordan Whitfield.

No Linear tickets were created for Keiko Tanaka, Rachel Dominguez, Sasha Greenwald, or Jordan Whitfield, all of which showed 'In Progress' status in the Airtable data.
